title: Grew Out of Evangelion Lol
wikitext:
[]: Grew Out of Evangelion Lol             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Jan 04, 2008 11:39 pm No, I don't mean myself. I mean someone who isn't me (and who isn't the First Ancestral Race). I'm referring to my sister, whose identity I will not reveal but whom I shall here call Helena. Yeah, so she first saw Neon Genesis Evangelion when she was ten years old (by which I mean she saw the Platinum series through episode 24 and then saw End of Evangelion ) and she loved it. It was apparently her favorite anime and she referred to it to a friend as that show she "was obsessed with". When asked whether she preferred NGE or one of her other favorite shows, Teen Titans , she said that she prefered Evangelion by far. N. E. Waize, she's twelve now, and she started rewatching the series recently and mentioned that she didn't like it that much anymore and was frequently bored. She didn't finish watching it. We are both somewhat confused by this. To get a sample of her current tastes in anime off the top of my head, her favorite anime is Fullmetal Alchemist , which she watched twice; she also loves Ouran High School Host Club , which she watched thrice; she didn't like Hellgirl that much; she likes FLCL ; she disikes shows like Naruto and Dragonball Z ; she felt He is My Master was okay enough to watch through once, but not all that good; she liked the first two episodes of Mahoromatic but was not allowed to watch the rest of the show. She liked Azumanga Daioh , but has so far only seen it once, with the exception of the first episode. She saw the first episode of Lucky Star and couldn't get into it. Finally, she recently watched Full Moon wo Sagashite , which she really liked except for the ending, which mildly perturbed her. So, these likes and dislikes should build a full psychological model of "Helena"'s character. Any thoughts as to why she "outgrew" NGE? :o

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Fri Jan 04, 2008 11:41 pm Sooner or later, many people here are going to get bored of it. The only people excepting this are those hard core fans like Eva Monkey and Mr Tines.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Jan 04, 2008 11:52 pm She likes the sillier anime's as oppossed to the more serious?

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Fri Jan 04, 2008 11:54 pm Navery, I think your sister clearly has outgrown "the first part" of Evangelion, you know, the action/mecha part she is not yet "grown (or emotionally/psychologically damaged)" enough to appreciate "the second part", the existential/psychological part she currently considers boring

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Jan 04, 2008 11:59 pm Eva is great, but not the end-all of animu. She probably has found things that fall in line better with her current tastes. She may or may not come back to Eva. As long as she enjoyed it, that should be good enough.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Jan 05, 2008 12:24 am Eh? "Good enough?" I don't really care whether she shares all my interests, if that's what you mean. I don't necessarily feel like if I really like something, those close to me should also like it. If, on the other hand, you're saying something involving Kaworu being female, then I'll hear nothing of it. (Oh yeah, and I forgot to mention that she liked Melody of Oblivion . Yes, she watches a lot of anime. Seriously, it's at least a three-to-one ratio to live action shows.)

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Jan 05, 2008 12:27 am NAveryW wrote: Eh? "Good enough?" I don't really care whether she shares all my interests, if that's what you mean. I don't necessarily feel like if I really like something, those close to me should also like it. If, on the other hand, you're saying something involving Kaworu being female, then I'll hear nothing of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Sat Jan 05, 2008 1:00 am she felt He is My Master was okay enough to watch through once, but not all that good; she liked the first two episodes of Mahoromatic but was not allowed to watch the rest of the show

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Jan 05, 2008 1:14 am Defectron wrote: from what I've seen of he is my master Mahoromatic looks to be the tamer of the two shows

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Sat Jan 05, 2008 1:17 am Yeah he is my master was so retardedly stupid I could only sit through the first episode of it I consider it to be the worst gainax anime (although I suppose its possible it got better after I stopped watching it). So yeah I didn't see that much of it, it just looked like it was going to be uber echii from the first episode which is why I thought that.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Jan 05, 2008 1:19 am NAveryW wrote: HiMM contains no frontal nudity.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jan 05, 2008 4:21 am BEsERk EVA01 wrote: Sooner or later, many people here are going to get bored of it. The only people excepting this are those hard core fans like Eva Monkey and Mr Tines.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Sat Jan 05, 2008 4:27 am Sorry, sir. But my point was those really hard core fans.

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Sat Jan 05, 2008 11:01 am Mr. Tines wrote: BEsERk EVA01 wrote: Sooner or later, many people here are going to get bored of it. The only people excepting this are those hard core fans like Eva Monkey and Mr Tines.

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Sat Jan 05, 2008 11:19 am BrikHaus wrote: NAveryW wrote: Eh? "Good enough?" I don't really care whether she shares all my interests, if that's what you mean. I don't necessarily feel like if I really like something, those close to me should also like it. If, on the other hand, you're saying something involving Kaworu being female, then I'll hear nothing of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Sat Jan 05, 2008 1:05 pm Well she's a kid, so it's not really my thing to ponder. But how about she thought it was cool and interesting at first, second time around it got longwinded?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Jan 05, 2008 1:08 pm Defectron wrote: Yeah he is my master was so retardedly stupid I could only sit through the first episode of it I consider it to be the worst gainax anime (although I suppose its possible it got better after I stopped watching it).

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Sat Jan 05, 2008 1:20 pm Didn't the maids in the end grew to love him though....?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Jan 05, 2008 2:11 pm Themaninblack wrote: Though Although its western when it comes to the comics/manga/cartoon/anime Watchmen has always beat Evangelion in my mind. Always.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Jan 05, 2008 2:19 pm Themaninblack wrote: Didn't the maids in the end grew to love him though....?
